title: The Ring Around the Stone
author: Town Records, Vol. I
text: The well came first. This is agreed upon by every account, which should itself arouse suspicion — Emberhollow's accounts agree on nothing else.
The founders did not dig the well. They found it, already old, already cold, already sweet. The town grew around the water like a ring around a stone.
What the founders were doing this far from anywhere, the records decline to say.

title: Charter of the Night Watch
author: Serjeant's Office
hint: Kept near the tower, where the Watch keeps its papers.
text: Article One: The Watch shall walk the roads from dusk-bell to dawn-bell, without exception.
Article Two: Reports of lights, sounds, or persons beyond the north wall shall be filed in triplicate and acted upon never.
Article Three: Lunch is sacred.
A later hand has added, in the margin: 'Article Two was a mistake. We should have looked.'

title: The Miller's Last Ledger
author: Unsigned
hint: Among the rosebushes, where the miller's garden stood.
text: The final entries are ordinary. Sacks in, flour out, a note about a squeaking bearing.
The last line reads: 'Grinding stopped at midday. Not by my hand. Will inspect the stones tomorrow.'
There is no entry for tomorrow. The mill has been quiet since, and Martena buys her flour from a cart that comes at dawn and does not linger.

title: A Taxonomy of Things With Teeth
author: Capt. E. Brammel (annotated)
hint: The Captain reads on the shore between casts.
text: A fisherman's guide to the local waters: herring (harmless), bass (spirited), perch (suspicious of everything, and rightly so).
The final chapter concerns a fourth thing, which takes lines, hooks, and on one occasion most of a net. The author has crossed out three proposed names for it.
The current annotation reads only: 'BIG. Generous lately. Why?'

title: What I Saw From the Wall
author: Shea, in a young hand
hint: Tucked by the northern walls, where a boy once kept lookout.
text: I counted nine lights in the hills tonight. Lanterns swing when a man walks. These did not swing.
They moved the way the water moves under ice — slow, and certain, and underneath.
Da says never mind the lights. Da says a lot of things in a voice that means he has seen them too.

title: Fragment: On the Door That Walks
author: Torn from a larger work
hint: The western fields keep what the cave discards.
text: …is not a cave at all, in the sense that a cave is a place. It is better understood as a visit.
It favors the observant. This is not kindness. A door prefers to be seen by those who might open it.
The shards it gives are pieces of somewhere else. Collect enough, and the somewhere else may notice the collection…
The remainder of the page is burned.

title: Songs Nobody Wrote
author: Collected at the tavern
hint: Where songs are sung and tabs are forgotten.
text: Every town has songs older than its memory. Emberhollow's are stranger than most.
There is the one about the wedding at the bottom of the well. The one that counts backward from nine and stops at four. The one about the miller's tomorrow.
Sung together, in order, they are said to make a fourth song. The tavern keeps them out of order on purpose.

title: The Baker's Almanac
author: Passed hand to hand
hint: Near the market stalls, dusted with flour.
text: Rise before the sun; the dough forgives nothing.
Well-water only. Cart-flour is fine, whatever anyone implies about the cart.
A margin note in Martena's hand: 'The almanac is wrong about only one thing. The dough forgives everything. That's why we make it.'

title: Inventory of the North Wall
author: Mason's Guild
hint: The masons left more than stone at the wall.
text: Stones: four thousand and change. Mortar: sufficient. Gates: none.
The absence of gates was deliberate and is the only line item marked PAID IN FULL twice.
The guild's final invoice includes a charge for 'facing the stones inward,' which no mason interviewed since will explain.

title: A Child's Primer of Runes
author: Author unknown, edition unknown
hint: Lost among the pines, where children were told not to play.
text: The first rune means WATER and is safe to draw.
The second rune means DOOR and should be drawn only in daylight.
The third rune means the thing that is drawn on the shards. Do not draw the third rune. This primer will not show you the third rune.
The rest of the primer is, of course, blank.
